results.title: Scientists may have finally solved the problem of the universe’s 'missing' black holes
results.author: Ben Turner
results.summary: Primordial black holes are one of the strongest candidates for the universe's missing dark matter. But a new theory suggests that not enough of the miniature black holes formed for this to be the case.

results.title: Stephen Hawking's black hole radiation paradox could finally be solved — if black holes aren't what they seem
results.author: Andrey Feldman
results.summary: New research suggests that black holes may actually be "frozen stars," bizarre quantum objects that lack a singularity and an event horizon, potentially solving some of the biggest paradoxes in black hole physics.

results.title: Astronomers find hundreds of 'hidden' black holes — and there may be billions or even trillions more
results.author: Joanna Thompson
results.summary: Black holes that have been obscured by clouds of dust still emit infrared light, enabling astronomers to spot them for the very first time.

results.title: The rarest black holes in the universe may be 'wandering' our galaxy — but scientists don't know how to detect them
results.author: Paul Sutter
results.summary: Dozens of 'wandering' black holes could be tumbling through our galaxy right now, new simulations hint. Their existence could help solve a longstanding cosmic puzzle.

results.title: Scientists discover bizarre region around black holes that proves Einstein right yet again
results.author: Ben Turner
results.summary: Einstein's general theory of relativity predicted that so-called 'plunging regions' around black holes would accelerate matter into them at the speed of light. Now, X-ray observations of a remote black hole have proved him right.
